CITY OF SAN MATEO
City Council Regular Meeting
July 18, 2022
7:00 PM
City Hall Council Chamber
330 West 20th Avenue
San Mateo CA 94403
COUNCIL MEMBERS
Rick Bonilla, Mayor
Diane Papan, Deputy Mayor
Joe Goethals
Amourence Lee
Eric Rodriguez
AGENDA
THIS MEETING IS BEING HELD PURSUANT TO THE GOVERNOR’S EXECUTIVE ORDERS
WHICH SUSPEND CERTAIN REQUIREMENTS OF THE BROWN ACT.
THIS MEETING CAN BE ATTENDED IN PERSON OR REMOTE BY JOINING ZOOM – SEE CODES BELOW.
PUBLIC COMMENTS WILL BE ACCEPTED BOTH IN PERSON AND REMOTELY.
SEE END OF AGENDA FOR OPTIONS AND INSTRUCTIONS ON HOW TO PARTICIPATE IN THE MEETING.
To join via Zoom – click here: July 18, 2022
To join via telephone: (408) 638-0968
Webinar ID: 816 6009 3523
Passcode: 533702
Deputy Mayor Diane Papan will teleconference into this meeting from:
1354 North Bundy, Los Angeles CA 90049
The City Council meeting will conclude by 11:00 p.m. unless otherwise extended by council vote.
Any unheard items will automatically move forward to the next regular meeting.
CALL TO ORDER
Pledge of Allegiance
Roll Call
CEREMONIAL
1.
Deputy City Clerk Joan Diskin – Commendatory Retirement Resolution
2.
RethinkWaste 2022 Trash to Art Winner Recognition
CONSENT CALENDAR
All matters listed under the Consent Calendar are considered by the Council to be routine and will be enacted by one motion without
discussion. If discussion is desired, that item may be removed and considered separately.
3.
City Council 2023 Regular Meeting Calendar – Adoption
Adopt the City Council Regular Meeting Schedule for calendar year 2023.
4.
Public Works and Library Departments – Classification Updates
Adopt a Resolution to approve the: 1) job specifications and salary ranges for two new classifications of
Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) Technicians; 2) addition of five WWTP positions and two Public Works
Engineering Department positions; 3) elimination of three WWTP positions; 4) revisions to the job specifications
for various classifications in the WWTP and in the Engineering Division; and 5) reallocation of a vacant Library
Assistant position to an Administrative Technician classification in the Library Department.
1 of 615

--- page 2 ---
5.
Juvenile Diversion Program Case Management Services – Contract and Amendment
Approve a three year contract with the Youth Service Bureau of the YMCA of San Francisco for Juvenile Diversion
Program case management services in the total amount of $384,715; approve Amendment No. 4 to the
agreement with the County of San Mateo to increase the reimbursement amount by $100,000 for case
management services for the San Mateo Police Department Juvenile Diversion Program; and authorize the Chief
of Police to execute the documents in substantially the form presented.
6.
Unpaid Code Enforcement Abatement Costs and Citations - 2022 Proposed Property Tax Liens
Approve the list of properties proposed for tax liens for unpaid Code Enforcement abatement costs and citations,
and direct staff to forward the list to the San Mateo County Controller’s Office to be included in the current year
tax roll.
7.
2020 Bicycle Master Plan – Amendment
Adopt a Resolution to amend the City of San Mateo 2020 Bicycle Master Plan Citywide Bicycle Network.
8.
Citywide Street Rehabilitation Package 3-B (Phase 2) – Agreement
Approve an agreement with G. Bortolotto & Company, Inc. for the Citywide Street Rehabilitation Package 3-B
(Phase 2) Project in the amount of $1,755,339.50; establish a contingency reserve in the amount of $176,000 for
agreement; and authorize the Public Works Director to execute the agreement in substantially the form
presented and issue change orders within the contingency reserve.
9.
Pavement Management and Smooth Streets Program Management Services – Agreement
Approve an agreement with Freyer & Laureta, Inc. for Program Management Services for the Smooth Streets and
Pavement Management programs in an amount not to exceed $200,000; establish a contingency of $20,000; and
authorize the Public Works Director to execute the amendment in substantially the form presented and issue
change orders within the contingency amount.
10.
High Voltage Streetlight Conversion (Phase 4) Design Services – Agreement
Approve an agreement with HDR Engineering, Inc. for design services for High Voltage Streetlight Conversion
(Phase 4) in an amount not to exceed $259,400; establish a contingency reserve of $25,000; and authorize the
Public Works Director to execute the agreement in substantially the form presented and approve amendments
within the contingency amount.
11.
Citywide Street Reconstruction Phase 3 – Agreements and Budget Transfer
Adopt a Resolution to authorize a budget transfer from Citywide Street Reconstruction Package 2 to Package 1 in
the amount of $2,900,000; approve an agreement with Teichert Construction for the Citywide Street
Reconstruction Phase 3 Project in the amount of $4,229,667 and establish a contingency reserve of $423,000;
approve an agreement with Ghirardelli Associates for construction inspection support services in the amount of
$150,000 and establish a contingency reserve of $15,000; and authorize the Public Works Director to execute the
agreements in substantially the form presented and issue change orders within the contingency amounts.
12.
Citywide Street Reconstruction Phase 3 Project – Amendment
Approve Amendment No. 4 to the agreement with Wilsey Ham for engineering services during construction in an
amount not to exceed $134,000 for a new agreement total of $528,874; and authorize the Public Works Director
to execute the amendment in substantially the form presented.

13.
Right-of-Way Permitting Support Services – Agreement
Approve an agreement with Anchor Engineering to provide right-of-way permitting review and processing
services in the amount of $500,000; and authorize the Director of Public Works to execute the agreement in
substantially the form presented.
14.
2750 South Delaware Street Sidewalk Easement – Acceptance
Adopt a Resolution to accept a Sidewalk Easement for Bay Meadows Station Block 1, 2750 South Delaware Street,
in substantially the form presented.
15.
2750 South Delaware Street Access Easement – Acceptance
Adopt a Resolution to accept an Access Easement on 2750 South Delaware Street in substantially the form
presented.
16.
2022 Annual Sewer Basin Rehabilitation Projects – Agreements
Approve four on-call professional design services agreements for the Clean Water Program with CSG Consultants,
Schaaf & Wheeler, Wilsey Ham, and Freyer & Laureta for various sewer pipe rehabilitation projects in amounts
not-to-exceed $1,500,000 for each consultant; and authorize the Public Works Director to execute the
agreements in substantially the form presented and issue project-specific task orders up to the not-to-exceed
amount under each agreement.
17.
Local Roadway Safety Plan – Agreement
Approve an agreement with Kittelson & Associates, Inc. for preparation of the San Mateo Local Roadway Safety
Plan in the amount of $120,938; approve a contingency in the amount of $12,000; and authorize the Public Works
Director to execute the agreement and amendments within the contingency in substantially the form presented.
18.
Rail Shuttle Bus Service Administration – Agreements
Approve the Amended and Restated Rail Shuttle Bus Service Administration Agreements for the Campus Drive
and Norfolk Shuttles with Commute.org for extension of shuttle operations for a total of $73,375 and authorize
the Public Works Director to execute the agreement in substantially the form presented.
19.
SF-Peninsula Regional Purewater Project – Memorandum of Agreement
Adopt a Resolution to approve a Memorandum of Agreement between the Bay Area Water Supply and
Conservation Agency, California Water Service Company, City and County of San Francisco through its Public
Utilities Commission, Mid-Peninsula Water District, and Silicon Valley Clean Water for the development of a basis
of design report for the SF-Peninsula Regional Purewater project, which will incorporate the City as a party to the
agreement and commit to a cost share not to exceed $100,000; and authorize the City Manager to execute the
agreement in substantially the form presented.
PUBLIC COMMENT
Members of the public wishing to comment on any item not appearing on the agenda may address the City Council at this time. State
law prevents Council from taking action on any matter not on the agenda; your comments may be referred to staff for follow up.
Public comment is limited to a total of 15 minutes; however, an opportunity for additional public comment may be provided later in
the agenda.
OLD BUSINESS
20.
General Plan Update – Land Use Map
Confirm the draft land use map and the heights and densities associated with each land use category for the
General Plan Update, and consider a land use designation request at 1501-1555 Hillsdale Blvd.

Ordinance Introduction – “Minimum Wage” Amendments

percentage cap, a catch-up provision, and round the minimum wage to the nearest five cents.

fiscal year 2022-23 Special Tax Levy, and approving and authorizing related actions and documents.

Public Employee Pension Reform Act 180 Day Waiting Period Waiver Request – Finance Department
Adopt a Resolution finding the nature of employment for retired annuitant Dr. Bhavna Chaudhary is necessary to
fill a critically needed position before 180 days have passed pursuant to California Government Code section
7522.56.

City Manager, City Attorney and Council Members report on their various assignments and liaison roles and Council requests for
scheduling future items.

AGENDAS: Agendas and material are posted on the City’s website on the Friday preceding each Council Meeting and can be viewed on the City's
website at www.cityofsanmateo.org . Any supplemental material distributed to the Council after the posting of the agenda will be made part of
the official record.
WATCHING A MEETING ON TV: City Council meetings are broadcast live on Comcast/channel 27, Wave/channel 26, or AT&T/channel 99.
For transmission problems during the broadcast, please call (650) 522-7099. For all other broadcast comments, call (650) 522-7040, Monday-Friday,
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
WATCHING A MEETING ON A COMPUTER: There are three ways to stream.
1) Public Meeting Portal www.cityofsanmateo.org/publicmeetings
2) City YouTube channel and stream it on YouTube: http://youtube.com/CityofSanMateo
3) Watch TV live stream: https://www.cityofsanmateo.org/193/Channel-San-Mateo-Live-Stream
PUBLIC COMMENTS/REQUEST TO SPEAK
Prior to the Meeting
Send comments to: clerk@cityofsanmateo.org until 4 p.m. the day of the meeting.
During the meeting
By Zoom: Click the link at the top of the agenda and you’ll be added to the meeting. All attendees are muted by default. When the item of interest
is open for consideration, select the “Raise Your Hand” icon and you will be called on at the appropriate time.
By telephone: Call (408) 638-0968 and enter the conference ID found at the top of the meeting agenda. When the item of interest is open for
consideration, select *9 to raise your hand. When called upon, press *6 to unmute, state your name and provide your comments.
In Person: At the meeting complete a “Request to Speak” form, submit a request at the speaker kiosk or scan the QR code.
ACCESSIBILITY: In compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, those with disabilities requiring special accommodations to participate in
this meeting may contact the City Clerk’s Office at (650) 522-7040 or clerk@cityofsanmateo.org . Notification 48 hours prior to the meeting will
enable the City to make reasonable arrangements to ensure accessibility to this meeting.

City Clerk's Office
MEETING DATE:
July 18, 2022 
SUBJECT:
City Council 2023 Regular Meeting Calendar – Adoption
RECOMMENDATION:
Adopt the City Council Regular Meeting Schedule for calendar year 2023.
BACKGROUND:
Attached is the proposed calendar for Regular City Council meetings, which are held on the first and third Mondays of each 
month with the exceptions as noted, due to holidays. One regular meeting is scheduled for the months of July, August, and 
December.
Please note that the second meeting in June occurs on the new federal holiday of Juneteenth – Monday, June 19, 2023. In 
June 2022, the second June meeting fell on June 20th, which was the recognized day for celebrating Juneteenth. We heard 
from several members of the public concerned and expressing confusion that we were holding a meeting on a new federal 
holiday. It was explained that the City does not follow all the federal holidays, for instance Columbus Day. 
The Council could leave the calendar as is or direct staff to move the meeting to Tuesday, June 20th.  Council could also 
discuss and direct other options.  If Council wishes to discuss, this item should be removed from the Consent Calendar. If it 
is not removed, we will adopt the calendar as presented. 
BUDGET IMPACT:
There is no budget impact from this administrative decision.
ENVIRONMENTAL DETERMINATION:
This item is not a project subject to CEQA, because it is an organizational or administrative activity that will not result in 
direct or indirect physical changes in the environment. (CEQA Guidelines Section 15378(b)(5).)
NOTICE PROVIDED
All meeting noticing requirements were met.

TO:
City Council
FROM:
Drew Corbett, City Manager
PREPARED BY:
Human Resources Department
MEETING DATE:
July 18, 2022 
SUBJECT: Classification Updates – Department of Public Works and Library Department
RECOMMENDATION:
Adopt a Resolution to approve the: 1) job specifications and salary ranges for two new classifications of Wastewater 
Treatment Plant (WWTP) Technicians; 2) addition of five WWTP positions and two Public Works Engineering Department 
positions; 3) elimination of three WWTP positions; 4) revisions to the job specifications for various classifications in the 
WWTP and in the Engineering Division; and 5) reallocation of a vacant Library Assistant position to an Administrative 
Technician classification in the Library Department.
BACKGROUND:
Per San Mateo Municipal Code Section 2.57.030(d), classification updates become effective upon approval by the City 
Council.
PUBLIC WORKS DEPARTMENT:
Wastewater Treatment Plant
A technical staffing derivation developed for the City by a Wastewater Treatment Plant optimization consultant 
determined that based on the significant increase in Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) assets requiring maintenance, 
new classifications and additional staff would be necessary to adequately maintain the new WWTP.  At the April 4, 2022 
study session, the City Council supported increased staffing at the WWTP to meet the future needs under the Clean Water 
Program. The Public Works Department proposes the following classification updates:
1.
Create a Senior Instrument Control Technician classification
Senior Instrument Control Technician is the advanced level of the existing Instrument Control Technician classification and 
is proposed to monitor, fix, adjust and oversee contractors in relation to the WWTP’s Supervisory Control and Data 
Acquisition (SCADA) system. This position will perform lead duties over the Instrument Control Technician and proposed 
new Calibration Technician and will provide oversight responsibilities and focus on SCADA systems. In maintaining our 
internal salary relationships, the recommended monthly salary range for Senior Instrument Control Technician is $9,120.20 
– $10,405.82, which is 10% above the salary range for Instrument Control Technician.
2.
Create a Calibration Technician classification
The Calibration Technician is the entry level of the existing Instrument Control Technician classification and will be 
responsible for calibrating, testing, and maintaining the large number of data acquisition, radio communications, and 
instrumentation equipment at the WWTP.  This position will have limited focus on controls and SCADA systems. In 
7 of 615

--- page 8 ---
CITY OF SAN MATEO
maintaining our internal salary relationships, the recommended monthly salary range for Calibration Technician is 
$7,461.98 – $8,513.86, which is 10% below the salary range for Instrument Control Technician.
3.
Add 3.0 merit FTEs Wastewater Treatment Plant Mechanic I-II
The addition of 3.0 FTEs will bring the total number of WWTP Mechanic I-II positions to six and will be organized into two 
teams and overseen by two WWTP Mechanic IIIs.
4.
Add 1.0 merit FTE Wastewater Treatment Plant Mechanic III
The addition of 1.0 FTE will bring the total number of WWTP Mechanic III positions to two and will oversee a team of three 
WWTP Mechanic I-IIs, providing two four-person mechanic teams to address the maintenance needs at the WWTP.
5.
Add 1.0 merit FTE Facilities Maintenance Worker
The Facilities Maintenance Worker is an existing classification within the City but a new role at the WWTP and will be 
responsible for performing semi-skilled and skilled maintenance and repair work.
6.
Eliminate 2.0 merit FTEs Wastewater Treatment Plant Shift Supervisor
The elimination of 2.0 FTEs Wastewater Treatment Plant Shift Supervisor supports the actual operational practices at the 
WWTP and future needs based on the technical staffing derivation. The WWTP has two day and night shifts, overseen by 
two Shift Supervisors.  The City has never staffed up to the allocated 4.0 FTEs and does not intend to, allowing budget 
savings by eliminating two unutilized FTEs.  
7.
Eliminate 1.0 merit FTE Wastewater Treatment Plant Operator III
The elimination of 1.0 FTE WWTP Operator III is consistent with the technical staffing derivation and the current and 
planned operational practices, allowing budget savings by eliminating one unutilized FTE.  
8.
Update the Job Specifications for the following classifications
Updates to the job specifications for the classifications of WWTP Manager, Laboratory Supervisor, and Regulatory 
Compliance Manager reflect the accreditation requirements under the National Environmental Laboratory Accreditation 
Conference (NELAC) Institute for environmental laboratory oversight and management, and the duties performed in these 
positions. The updates to the job specifications for the classifications of WWTP Operations Superintendent, Instrument 
Control Technician, WWTP Mechanic III and Facilities Maintenance Worker are proposed to accurately reflect the 
reporting structure, essential duties, and change the certification from mandatory to preferred to improve recruitment 
and increase the pool of potential candidates. 
Engineering Services
9.
Add 1.0 merit FTE Junior/Assistant/Associate Engineer
The addition of 1.0 FTE Junior/Assistant/Associate Engineer is proposed to sufficiently provide adequate support in the 
Private Development section working on building permits and planning applications.
10. Add 1.0 merit FTE Administrative Assistant
The addition of 1.0 FTE Administrative Assistant is proposed to support the growing needs for administrative support in 
the Private Development section.
11. Update the job specification for the classification of Junior/Assistant/Associate Engineer
Updates to the job specification for the classification of Junior/Assistant/Associate Engineer reflect the revisions made in 
2017 by the Analytical Technical focus group as part of the core competency project to more accurately reflect the duties 
performed in the position.
LIBRARY DEPARTMENT:
The Library Department has operational needs to add 1.0 FTE Administrative Technician. The Main Library, along with the 
Hillsdale and Marina Library branches, are currently some of the most widely used public facilities in the City of San Mateo. 
With the increased community usage and continued expansion of library services to the community, administrative 
8 of 615

--- page 9 ---
CITY OF SAN MATEO
responsibilities in the Library Department continue to experience growth without the necessary administrative office staff 
support to meet service demands. Changes in technology, library operations, and facility maintenance necessitate the 
creation of this new role. The Administrative Technician position will fulfill a key goal of the Library’s strategic plan to 
provide staff with meaningful professional growth opportunities since this position is a bridging classification between the 
Executive Assistant and Management Analyst positions, both of which already exist at the Library. To add 1.0 FTE 
Administrative Technician position, the Library Department will reallocate a merit Library Assistant I-II position that has 
been vacant since January 2020 and eliminate two vacant per diem positions; Library Assistant I and Library Aide. 
BUDGET IMPACT:
For the classification updates in the Public Works Department, budget appropriations are reflected in the fiscal year 2022-
23 budget and no supplemental appropriation is required. There is no budget impact associated with the updates to the 
job specifications.
For the classification updates in the Library Department, the Administrative Technician’s annual, fully loaded cost is 
approximately $34,000 higher than the total of the reallocation and elimination of the Librarian Assistant I/II and two per 
diem positions. This amount can be absorbed in the Department's fiscal year 2022-23 operating budget. 
ENVIRONMENTAL DETERMINATION:
These classification updates are exempt from CEQA review because it can be seen with certainty that there is no possibility 
that this activity may have a significant effect on the environment. (CEQA Guidelines Section 15061(b)(3).)
NOTICE PROVIDED
All meeting noticing requirements were met.

CITY OF SAN MATEO
RESOLUTION NO. __ (2022)
CLASSIFICATION UPDATES IN THE DEPARTMENTS OF PUBLIC WORKS AND LIBRARY
WHEREAS, pursuant to San Mateo Municipal Code Section 2.57.030(d), approval of new classifications 
and related job specifications, salary schedules, compensation changes, elimination or reinstatement of 
positions, reallocation of positions, reclassifications, and revisions of job specifications must be approved by City 
Council Resolution; and
WHEREAS, the proposed changes as detailed below are in response to the 1) staffing derivation 
developed by a Wastewater Treatment Plant optimization consultant for the Public Works Department, 2) 
operational needs to provide adequate support in the Private Development section in the Engineering Services 
Division of the Public Works Department, and 3) changes in technology, operations, and facility maintenance in 
the Library Department; and
WHEREAS, the proposed and updated job specifications for the classifications of Senior Instrument 
Control Technician, Calibration Technician, WWTP Manager, Laboratory Supervisor, Regulatory Compliance 
Manager, WWTP Operations Superintendent, Instrument Control Technician, WWTP Mechanic III, Facilities 
Maintenance Worker, and Junior/Assistant/Associate Engineer are attached hereto as Exhibit A; the Public Works 
Department FY 2022-23 FTEs is attached as Exhibit B; the Library Department FY 2022-23 FTEs is attached as 
Exhibit C; and the Merit Salary Schedules for the classifications of Senior Instrument Control Technician and 
Calibration Technician are attached as Exhibit D and incorporated by reference; and
WHEREAS, the employee associations representing the impacted classifications have been informed of 
the classification updates and have not brought forth any objections or concerns; and
WHEREAS, this City Council action is exempt from CEQA review because it can be seen with certainty that 
there is no possibility that this activity may have a significant effect on the environment. (CEQA Guidelines 
Section 15061(b)(3).)
NOW, THEREFORE, THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF SAN MATEO, CALIFORNIA, HEREBY RESOLVES that 
the following classification updates are approved:
1.
The job specification and monthly salary range of $9,120.20 – $10,405.82 for the classification of 
Senior Instrument Control Technician,
2.
The job specification and monthly salary range of $7,461.98 – $8,513.86 for the classification of 
Calibration Technician,
3.
The addition of 3.0 merit FTEs Wastewater Treatment Plant Mechanic I-II,
4.
The addition of 1.0 merit FTE Wastewater Treatment Plant Mechanic III,
5.
The addition of 1.0 merit FTE Facilities Maintenance Worker,
6.
The addition of 1.0 merit FTE Junior/Assistant/Associate Engineer,
7.
The addition of 1.0 merit FTE Administrative Assistant,
10 of 615

--- page 11 ---
8.
The elimination of 2.0 merit FTEs Wastewater Treatment Plant Supervisor,
9.
The elimination of 1.0 merit FTE Wastewater Treatment Plant Operator III,
10. The revisions to the job specifications for the classifications of WWTP Manager, Laboratory 
Supervisor, Regulatory Compliance Manager, WWTP Operations Superintendent, Instrument Control 
Technician, WWTP Mechanic III, Facilities Maintenance Worker, and Junior/Assistant/Associate 
Engineer, and
11. The reallocation of a vacant 1.0 merit FTE Library Assistant I-II to the Administrative Technician 
classification.
11 of 615

--- page 12 ---
Exhibit A
SENIOR INSTRUMENT CONTROL TECHNICIAN
DEFINITION
To install, maintain, calibrate, test and repair a variety of data acquisition, radio communications, and 
instrumentation and control equipment for wastewater collection and treatment plant systems; Principal 
oversight of Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) computer systems, Programmable 
Logic Controllers (PLC). Assist in the oversight of the Computerized Maintenance Management 
Systems (CMMS).  Perform highly skilled electrical, electronic and mechanical work related to in-plant 
metering equipment and remote controlled devices; to operate, monitor and control an advanced 
wastewater treatment (tertiary) system; and to perform other related work as required. 
SUPERVISION RECEIVED AND EXERCISED
Receives general supervision from the Wastewater Treatment Plant Maintenance Superintendent.
Performs lead duties over the Instrumentation Controls Technician and Calibration Technician.
May provide technical or functional supervision related to the impacted systems to Plant operations or 
maintenance personnel, contractors or other designed personnel as required.
EXAMPLES OF ESSENTIAL DUTIES - Duties may include, but are not limited to, the following:
Assist in the installation, calibration, maintenance and troubleshooting of electronic and 
electromechanical equipment to ensure proper operation; assist in the troubleshooting and repairing of 
control system and electrical power failures in the WWTP, pump and lift station.
Install, calibrate, maintain, troubleshoot and repair electronic and electromechanical equipment to 
ensure proper operation; maintain and troubleshoot computerized process control equipment such as 
PLCs, RTUs, and SCADA system devices; troubleshoot control system and electrical power system 
failures in plants, lifts and pump stations; and maintain awareness of new related technology.
Develops and implements improvement projects for the instrumentation and controls systems and 
equipment by contractors or in-house staff. 
12 of 615

--- page 13 ---
Maintain documentation and records for the department’s SCADA system and 
instrumentation/control systems at the wastewater treatment plant (schematics, licenses, etc.). Perform 
regular technical programming of the SCADA system to provide programming and troubleshooting 
assistance as needed during special testing and operations. Confer with operators, mechanics, project 
engineers and supervisors regarding SCADA issues.
Respond to and resolve internal customer complaints and issues; provide documentation as required. 
Communicate in advance with affected staff on programming and related SCADA work.
Provide oversight, technical support, inspection and coordination over control system modification made 
by contractors. Maintain documentation and plans of modifications. 
Prepare reports on technical issues as required.
Develop, implement, and maintain procedures for documenting activities in instrumentation and 
control areas related to programming of RTUs and HMI software, field implementation of 
programming change, and process control improvements.
Provides safety and skills training to apprentice instrumentation technicians, electrician and other plant 
staff.
Write basic PLC programming for the implementation of system modifications and process 
enhancements. Interpret, prepare, and verify control schematics, diagrams and prints. Provide 
technical assistance and guidance to staff. Program control systems, instruments and computer 
operated devices.
Read, understand and perform minor modifications to existing PLC code. Download PLC programs to 
PLCs and RTUs.
Perform as lead person on Instrumentation/ Controls projects
Effectively provide training and direction to the Instrument Control Technician, calibration Technician 
and other plant staff. 
Install, troubleshoot, modify, and implement computer hardware and software applications and 
components.  Analyze and correct data communication and acquisition system problems.
Design, install, test, repair, calibrate and maintain complex electrical, electronic and mechanical 
equipment such as meters, telemeters, integrators, tone control equipment, temperature control 
devices, fire alarm equipment, water quality analyzers, life safety equipment, and remote controlling 
devices, including relays, rectifiers, and manometers.
Perform related duties as assigned.
13 of 615

--- page 14 ---
MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS
Knowledge of:
Principles of computer systems, procedures, analysis and design as related to the identified equipment and 
systems.
Programming languages, computer logic, query/analysis tools and mathematics.
Microsoft office 365, Visual Basic, PC applications and local area network are highly desirable.
Change management practices.
SCADA and internet security practices as it applies to an operational technology environment. 
Principles of budget monitoring.
Principles and practices of project management.
Advanced knowledge of SCADA systems, process controls, programmable controllers and related 
computers.
Standards, practices, methods, materials, tools and measuring instruments used in the electrical, 
electronic and mechanical fields.
Principles and practices of safety management.
Pertinent local, State and Federal laws, codes, ordinances and regulations.            
Ability to:
Monitor and direct contractors, record system changes.

Develop and implement various data collection and reporting systems.

CALIBRATION TECHNICIAN
DEFINITION
To install, maintain, calibrate, test and repair a variety of data acquisition, radio communications, and 
instrumentation for wastewater collection and treatment plant systems; to assist in the oversight of 
Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) computer systems, Programmable Logic 
Controllers (PLC) to perform skilled electrical, electronic and mechanical work related to in-plant 
metering equipment and remote controlled devices; to operate, monitor and control an advanced 
wastewater treatment (tertiary) system; and to perform other related work as required.
SUPERVISION RECEIVED AND EXERCISED
Receives general supervision from the Wastewater Treatment Plant Maintenance Superintendent.
May provide technical or functional supervision related to the impacted systems to Plant operations or 
maintenance personnel, contractors or other designed personnel as required.
EXAMPLES OF ESSENTIAL DUTIES - Duties may include, but are not limited to, the following:
Assist in the installation, calibration, maintenance and troubleshooting of electronic and 
electromechanical equipment to ensure proper operation; assist in the troubleshooting and repairing of 
control system and electrical power failures in the WWTP, pump and lift station.
Install, calibrate, maintain, troubleshoot and repair electronic and electromechanical equipment to 
ensure proper operation; maintain and troubleshoot computerized process control equipment such as 
PLCs, RTUs, and SCADA system devices; troubleshoot control system and electrical power system 
failures in plants, lifts and pump stations; and maintain awareness of new related technology.
Confer with operators, mechanics, project engineers and supervisors regarding SCADA issues.
Respond to and resolve internal customer complaints and issues; provide documentation as required. 
Communicate in advance with affected staff on programming and related SCADA work.
Prepare reports on technical issues as required.
Develop, implement, and maintain procedures for documenting activities in instrumentation and 
17 of 615

--- page 18 ---
control areas related to programming of RTUs and HMI software, field implementation of 
programming change, and process control improvements.
Interpret, prepare, and verify control schematics, diagrams and prints. Provide technical assistance 
and guidance to staff. Program control systems, instruments and computer operated devices.
Install, troubleshoot, modify, and implement computer hardware and software applications and 
components.  Analyze and correct data communication and acquisition system problems.
Install, test, repair, calibrate and maintain complex electrical, electronic and mechanical equipment 
such as meters, temperature control devices, fire alarm equipment, and remote water quality analyzers 
controlling devices, including relays, rectifiers, and manometers.
Perform related duties as assigned.

WASTEWATER TREATMENT PLANT MANAGER
DEFINITION
To plan, manage, and supervise the operations, maintenance, and laboratory functions of the City's 
Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) using existing wastewater treatment processes, including primary, 
clarification, biological oxidation processes, solids dewatering, sludge digestion, secondary treatment 
membrane filtration, and effluent disinfection; and to do related work as required. 
SUPERVISION RECEIVED AND EXERCISED
Receives general direction from the Deputy Director of Public Works, Environmental Services Division.
Exercises direct supervision over operation and maintenance superintendents, laboratory supervisor, and 
professional, technical and clerical staff.
EXAMPLES OF ESSENTIAL DUTIES 
Duties may include, but are not limited to, the following:
Establish goals and objectives; assist in the development and implementation of policies and procedures; 
establish schedules and methods for the operation and maintenance of the plant.
Plan, supervise and manage the activities of a Grade V wastewater treatment plant.
 
Interpret policies and procedures established by county, regional, state and federal regulatory agencies to 
ensure compliance with all current waste discharge requirements.
Analyze plant and laboratory reports and direct the adjustment of processing methods to improve plant 
efficiency in coordination with the Operations Superintendent.
Prepare plant budget; assist in budget implementation; participate in the forecast of additional funds 
needed for staffing, equipment, materials, and supplies; administer the approved budget.
Participate in the long-term strategic planning efforts of the Environmental Services Division, 
representing the needs and interests of the wastewater treatment plant through development and 
implementation of a strategic plans within the Division.
Participate in recommending the appointment of, training, motivating and evaluating personnel; 
participate in establishing and monitoring employee performance objectives; prepare and present 
employee performance reviews; provide or coordinate staff training; work with employees to correct 
21 of 615

--- page 22 ---
deficiencies; implement discipline procedures; recommend employee terminations.
Control the procurement of supplies, materials and equipment.
Ensure the plant has an effective safety program, including the continuance of a functional Safety 
Committee.
Prepare staff reports for presentation to the City Council, Public Works Commission, City Manager or 
Public Works Director.
Manage and coordinate plant operations activities with the plant laboratory to ensure the proper, efficient, 
and integrity of total functioning plant equipment and wastewater quality control systems.
Oversee the overall operations, human resources, and service performance of the laboratory and ensures 
supervision by appropriately qualified staff in accordance with Environmental Laboratory Accreditation 
Program requirements.
Manage coordination with the Regulatory Compliance programs to ensure compliance of all plant 
operations with all federal, state, regional and local laws, regulations and permits.
Manage the regular review and update of standard operating procedures for Plant operations and 
maintenance to ensure compliance with environmental permits, OSHA safety standards and local codes 
and ordinances.
When the Chief Plant Operator (CPO) is unavailable, sign and file self-monitoring reports to the 
regulatory agencies.
Prepare, review and submit periodic reports regarding wastewater quality treatment.
Provide technical assistance to the Engineering and General Services Division; review and assist in the 
development of plans and specifications for treatment plant capital construction projects and equipment.
Make critical decisions to resolve emergency conditions resulting from wastewater treatment system 
malfunctions or upsets.
Fully utilize the computerized work systems in accordance with work flow processes.
Respond, track and resolve difficult citizen complaints and concerns.
Coordinate division activities with other City departments, divisions and sections and with other cities 
and outside agencies.
Perform related duties as assigned.
MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS
22 of 615

--- page 23 ---
Knowledge of:
Theory and practice of wastewater treatment and of machines, equipment and material used in 
such treatment.
Chemical and physical processes involved in clarification, activated sludge, biofiltration, 
disinfection, advanced methods of wastewater treatment, sludge thickening and dewatering, and 
biosolids disposal.
Disposal and reuse of wastewater residuals including but not limited to treated wastewater, bio-
solids and gas.
Occupational hazard correction and safety precautions necessary in work.
Applicable federal, state and regional laws and regulations.
Budgeting principles and techniques.
Use of Computerized Maintenance Management Systems to optimize preventative and other 
maintenance activities within a Wastewater Treatment Plant.
Principles and practices of project management.
Principles of organization, administration and personnel management.
Ability to:
Plan and organize the operation and maintenance of a wastewater treatment plant.
Assist in planning and organization of process improvements and plant upgrades so that 
existing processes and equipment are not negatively affected.
Analyze biological and physical processes and make adjustments to optimize waste treatment and 
disposal.
Use computers and handheld electronic devices in the course of work.
Prepare and supervise the preparation and maintenance of records and reports.
Communicate clearly and concisely, both orally and in writing.
Select, supervise, train and evaluate personnel.
Experience and Training
Any combination of experience and training that would provide the required knowledge and 
abilities is qualifying.  A typical way to obtain the required knowledge and abilities would be:
23 of 615

--- page 24 ---
Experience:
Five years of increasingly responsible experience in wastewater treatment plant operations, 
including four years of wastewater treatment plant operator qualifying experience with two years 
of supervisory experience.
Training:
Equivalent to a Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited college or university with major course work 
in civil or sanitary engineering, environmental sciences or a related field.
License or Certificate:
Possession of a Grade V Wastewater Treatment Plant Operator Certificate issued by the State of 
California, preferred (not required).
Or
Registration as a professional chemical, mechanical or civil engineer in the State of California, with the 
ability to obtain a Grade V Wastewater Treatment Plant Certificate within a minimal amount of time 
from date of appointment, preferred (not required).
Possession of, or ability to obtain, a valid California Class C driver’s license. 
ADA Special Requirement:
Essential duties require the following physical abilities and work environment:
Ability to work in a standard office environment with some ability to sit, stand, walk, kneel, crouch 
squat, stoop, reach crawl, twist climb, and lift 25 pounds, some exposure to extreme noise, outdoors, 
electrical hazards, vibration, chemicals, dust, toxic waste, and explosive materials.

LABORATORY SUPERVISOR
DEFINITION
To manage, supervise and participate in the activities of the Wastewater Treatment Plant Laboratory and 
Regulatory Compliance functions; to oversee and perform a variety of chemical, bacteriological and 
physical analyses of water, wastewater, storm water and receiving water; coordinate test results with 
operations staff, review regulatory requirements, facilitate compliance, participate in permit renewal, 
assure Environmental Laboratory Accreditation Program (ELAP) certification, represent the wastewater 
treatment plant (WWTP) at regulatory hearings as assigned and provide recommendations to the 
Regulatory Compliance Manager related to regulatory compliance; and to do related work as required.
SUPERVISION RECEIVED AND EXERCISED
Receives direction from the WWTP Manager, or higher-level department personnel.
Exercises direct supervision over Lab Analysts (I and II) and other staff as assigned.
EXAMPLES OF DUTIES - Duties may include, but are not limited to, the following:
Supervise laboratory operations and data reporting, including: 
Oversee all analytical and operational activities of the laboratory. 
Ensure accuracy and quality of all data reported by the laboratory. 
Monitor standards of performance for quality assurance and quality control.
Monitor the validity of the analyses performed, and data generated in the laboratory to assure reliable 
data.
Recommend and assist in the implementation of goals and objectives; establish schedules and methods 
for laboratory operations; implement policies and procedures; develop, revise and update laboratory 
standard operating procedures.
Supervise, prioritize, and schedule the work of laboratory personnel and monitor all laboratory operations 
for ELAP certification and wastewater and stormwater National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System 
(NPDES) permit requirements.
Supervise and participate in the collection, sampling and analysis of plant influent and effluent, plant 
processes, biosolids and other analysis as assigned for compliance with Regional Water Quality 
Control Board requirements, NPDES permits, and other regulations.
25 of 615

--- page 26 ---
Plan, implement and maintain a plant monitoring program; participate with operations staff to devise 
operational strategies that optimize plant performance.
Coordinate with Plant Manager and Plant Operations Superintendent on treatment plant monitoring and 
operations for NPDES permit compliance.
Participate in budget preparation and administration; prepare cost estimates for budget recommendations; 
submit justifications for needed materials and equipment; monitor and control expenditures.
Prepare and implement analysis and testing schedule; monitor test results and investigate and resolve 
inconsistent data.
Perform the duties of Laboratory Analysts during staffing shortages.
Maintain inventory of laboratory supplies and supervise the storage of hazardous materials related to 
laboratory functions.  Generate purchase requisitions for laboratory equipment, chemicals and other 
supplies, as needed.
Establish, implement and maintain a quality assurance program.  Serve as Laboratory Quality Assurance 
Officer and as the authorized signatory for the Quality Assurance program manual.
Maintain Chemical Hygiene Plan.  Serve as Laboratory Chemical Hygiene Officer.
 
Maintain, review and update detailed records, post analytical results, and prepare a variety of technical 
reports.  Supervise compliance with self-monitoring reports for the plant NPDES permit, and stormwater 
NPDES permit, as required.
Interface with Regional Water Quality Control Board staff, state and county health department staff and 
other regulatory agencies as necessary.
Participate in the selection, training, motivation and evaluation of staff; participate in monitoring 
employee performance objectives; prepare employee performance reviews; provide or coordinate staff 
training; work with employees to correct deficiencies; implement discipline procedures and maintain 
personnel documentation.
Research, plan and develop new laboratory techniques and evaluate new instruments.
Represent City at various workshops, conferences and board meetings as required.
Maintain laboratory equipment and perform minor repairs; coordinate additional repair work and  
modifications with maintenance staff or outside contractors.
Participate at a managerial level on various WWTP and departmental committees.

Proper water, wastewater and stormwater sampling and analyses techniques.
QUALIFICATIONS

Three years of increasingly responsible experience in an analytical laboratory performing analysis of 
water, wastewater, solid waste, hazardous waste or other environmental samples. A master's degree in

natural or physical science may be substituted for one (1) year of the required analysis experience. A 
doctorate in chemistry, biochemistry, environmental, sanitary or public health engineering, biology, 
microbiology, natural or physical science may be substituted for two (2) years of the required analysis

REGULATORY COMPLIANCE MANAGER
DEFINITION
Under general direction of the Deputy Director, manages and provides oversight in the planning and 
development, directing and implementation of regulatory programs within the Engineering and 
Environmental Services Divisions and with other departments. Provides representation with Regional and 
State regulatory agencies. Primary programs include:  Sanitary Sewer Pretreatment and Pollution 
Prevention; Stormwater National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) permit (Municipal 
Regional Permit, or MRP); Water Quality Control Plant’s NPDES General Permit for industrial stormwater 
discharges; Marina Lagoon Water Quality Management, air quality programs related to the Water Quality 
Control Plant and sanitary collection system and pumping stations.  Coordinates with the Operations 
Manager and Laboratory Supervisor in ensuring compliance with the Water Quality Control Plant NPDES 
permit.  Also performs work in connection with the solid waste program and other environmental programs 
and issues as assigned.
DISTINGUISHING CHARACTERISTICS
This classification is distinguished from all other City classifications having a regulatory emphasis through 
the specialized background required and its responsibilities for oversight, management, coordination and 
direction of assigned regulatory and environmental programs and sub-programs.
SUPERVISION RECEIVED AND EXERCISED
Receives policy direction and general supervision from the Engineering Services Deputy Director, or 
higher-level department personnel. 
Provides direct supervision to Waste Management Supervisor, Environmental Programs Coordinator, 
Environmental Compliance Inspectors, and other staff as assigned.
EXAMPLES OF ESSENTIAL DUTIES - Duties may include, but are not limited to, the following:
Plans, develops, and manages the implementation of programs and sub-programs relating to:


Wastewater pretreatment and pollution prevention 

Stormwater NPDES permit compliance 

Environmental management of the Marina Lagoon 

Water Quality Control Plant’s NPDES General Permit for industrial stormwater discharge
29 of 615

--- page 30 ---

Compliance with air quality regulations for the wastewater treatment plant collection system 
and pumping stations 

Solid waste management.
Develops and manages program standard operating procedures, goals, objectives and priorities; and 
monitors program implementation through development of approved standards of performance.
Plans, organizes, directs and reviews the activities of staff and professional services contracts as assigned, 
and assists in facilitating training and professional development of City staff involved in implementation 
of assigned programs.
Participates as a full member of the division senior management team to actively discuss issues, problems 
and alternative solutions; collegial problem solving with team members.
Participate with Deputy Director and division senior management team in long-term planning for annual, 
five-year, ten-year, and twenty-year planning horizons.
Performs ongoing supervising duties of assigned staff, including: assignment, prioritization and review of 
work; training; approval of time off; and the making of effective recommendations concerning hiring, 
promotion and disciplinary actions.
Prepares and administers operating budgets in connection with assigned programs, and the environmental 
compliance fee schedule.
Ensures that assigned programs are implemented and maintained in compliance with local, state and federal 
program mandates.
Conducts technical studies; and prepares administrative reports, amendments to policy documents and 
ordinances, periodic compliance reports to regulatory agencies and other correspondence.
Participates in the development of project specifications and in construction plan review in connection with 
wastewater pretreatment requirements and Best Management Practices (BMP’s) and other requirements 
for compliance with City’s stormwater NPDES permit.
Coordinates planning and implementation of assigned programs with other City departments and with 
regulatory agencies such as the Regional Water Quality Control Board, Bay Area Air Quality Management 
District, and San Mateo County Health Services Agency.
Acts as City representative for regulatory matters throughout multi-jurisdictional wastewater treatment and 
stormwater management service areas on interagency committees and with regulatory agencies in matters 
pertaining to assigned programs.
Supervises investigation, review and resolution of environmental complaints and instances of 
noncompliance, including gathering of evidence, issuing of compliance directives and initiating 
recommendations for referrals to the City Attorney or other appropriate enforcement authority as needed.
Initiates, oversees and/or conducts special studies to identify and characterize actual and potential pollutant 
sources and other technical studies as needed.
30 of 615

--- page 31 ---
Perform related duties as assigned.

Federal and state regulations and requirements as they relate to wastewater and stormwater

Principles and practices of project management.

applicable City policies and procedures.
Collect, analyze, interpret and apply a variety of data and information.

WASTEWATER TREATMENT PLANT OPERATIONS SUPERINTENDENT
DEFINITION
To assign, supervise, manage and review the work of the operations staff of the City's wastewater quality 
control and processing plant using existing wastewater treatment processes, including primary treatment, 
biological oxidation processes, physical and chemical clarification, solids dewatering, sludge digestion, 
secondary treatment, membrane filtration and disinfection; and to do related work as required.  
SUPERVISION RECEIVED AND EXERCISED
Receive general direction from the Wastewater Treatment Plant Manager, or higher-level department 
personnel
Exercises direct supervision over operations staff, including Shift Supervisors, Operators, Operators-in-
Training, and other professional and technical staff as may be required. 
EXAMPLES OF ESSENTIAL DUTIES 
Duties may include, but are not limited to, the following:
Act as and performs the duties of the designated Chief Plant Operator (CPO), and direct the activities of a 
Grade V wastewater treatment plant.
Develop and implement goals and objectives; establish schedules and methods for the operation of the 
plant; implement policies and procedures.
Assign, schedule and supervise employees in the operation of the treatment plant. Respond to 
emergencies on a 24-hour basis.
  
Maintain surveillance of the operating efficiency of wastewater treatment processes, including primary 
treatment, biological oxidation processes, secondary treatment, filtration, solids dewatering, sludge 
digestion, and effluent disinfection.
Assist in Operations Branch budget development and implementation; participate in the forecast of 
additional funds needed for staffing, equipment, materials and supplies; administer the approved budget.
Maintain records and prepare reports regarding the operation of a Grade V wastewater plant and quality 
of the effluent; monitor monthly reports to regulatory agencies.
34 of 615

--- page 35 ---
Recommend methods of correction of plant upsets and improving plant efficiency.
Coordinate plant operations activities with the plant laboratory and maintenance personnel to ensure the 
proper and efficient total functioning of plant equipment and wastewater quality control systems.
Ensure the Operations Department has an effective safety program.
Assist in selecting, training, motivating and evaluating operations personnel; participate in establishing 
and monitoring employee performance objectives; prepare and present employee performance reviews; 
provide or coordinate staff training; work with employees to correct deficiencies; implement discipline 
procedures; recommend employee terminations.
Prepare specifications for materials, supplies and contractual services including administrative Reports 
and Agreements for procurement of these materials, supplies and services in accordance with City 
purchasing policy.
Prepare staff reports for presentation to the City Council, Public Works Commission, City Manager or 
Public Works Director.
Review and approve request for proposal for the purchase of plant materials and supplies.
Fully utilize the computerized work systems in accordance with work flow processes.
Make critical decisions to resolve emergency conditions resulting from wastewater treatment system 
malfunctions, upsets or poisonings.
Ensure operations staff participation in Plant Capital Improvement Projects planning.
Coordinate operational activities with contractors of plant construction projects to ensure continuous 
plant operations.
Actively and collaboratively develop and implement long term strategic organizational and process 
planning for the WWTP Operations Branch.
Ensure appropriate communications at all levels of plant operations, including coordination with Plant 
Maintenance Superintendent, Laboratory Supervisor and regularly scheduled operations meetings.
May oversee Plant management functions in the absence of Wastewater Treatment Plant Manager.
Perform related duties as assigned.
MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS:
Knowledge of:
Thorough knowledge of operations of a Grade V wastewater treatment plant and good working 
knowledge of plant maintenance and laboratory functions.
Sewage treatment processes, chemical and bacteriological sewage characteristics, and standard methods 
of sampling and analyses of water and sewage.

Principles and practices of project management.
Principles and practices of supervision, training and performance evaluation.
Membrane Bioreactor Experience desirable

INSTRUMENT CONTROL TECHNICIAN
DEFINITION
To install, maintain, calibrate, test and repair a variety of data acquisition, radio communications, and 
instrumentation and control equipment for wastewater collection and treatment plant systems; to assist in 
the oversight of Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) computer systems, Programmable 
Logic Controllers (PLC) and Computerized Maintenance Management Systems (CMMS); to perform 
highly skilled electrical, electronic and mechanical work related to in-plant metering equipment and 
remote controlled devices; to operate, monitor and control an advanced wastewater treatment (tertiary) 
system; and to perform other related work as required.
SUPERVISION RECEIVED AND EXERCISED
Receives general supervision from the Wastewater Treatment Plant Maintenance Superintendent.
May provide technical or functional supervision related to the impacted systems to Plant operations or 
maintenance personnel, contractors or other designed personnel as required.
EXAMPLES OF ESSENTIAL DUTIES - Duties may include, but are not limited to, the following:
Assist in the installation, calibration, maintenance and troubleshooting of electronic and 
electromechanical equipment to ensure proper operation; assist in the troubleshooting and repairing of 
control system and electrical power failures in the WWTP, pump and lift station.
Install, calibrate, maintain, troubleshoot and repair electronic and electromechanical equipment to 
ensure proper operation; maintain and troubleshoot computerized process control equipment such as 
PLCs, RTUs, and SCADA system devices; troubleshoot control system and electrical power system 
failures in plants, lifts and pump stations; and maintain awareness of new related technology.
Maintain documentation and records for the department’s SCADA system and 
instrumentation/control systems  at  the  wastewater  treatment plant (schematics, licenses, etc.). 
Perform regular technical programming of the SCADA system to provide programming and 
troubleshooting assistance as needed during special testing and operations. Confer with operators, 
mechanics, project engineers and supervisors regarding SCADA issues.
Respond to and resolve internal customer complaints and issues; provide documentation as required. 
Communicate in advance with affected staff on programming and related SCADA work.
38 of 615

--- page 39 ---
Prepare reports on technical issues as required.
Develop, implement, and maintain procedures for documenting activities in instrumentation and 
control areas related to programming of RTUs and HMI software, field implementation of 
programming change, and process control improvements.
Write basic PLC programming for the implementation of system modifications and process 
enhancements. Interpret, prepare, and verify control schematics, diagrams and prints. Provide 
technical assistance and guidance to staff. Program control systems, instruments and computer 
operated devices.
Read, understand and perform minor modifications to existing PLC code. Download PLC programs to 
PLCs and RTUs.
Install, troubleshoot, modify, and implement computer hardware and software applications and 
components.  Analyze and correct data communication and acquisition system problems.
Design, install, test, repair, calibrate and maintain complex electrical, electronic and mechanical 
equipment such as meters, telemeters, integrators, tone control equipment, temperature control 
devices, fire alarm equipment, water quality analyzers, and remote controlling devices, including 
relays, rectifiers, and manometers.
Perform related duties as assigned.
MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS
Knowledge of:
Principles of computer systems, procedures, analysis and design as related to the identified equipment and 
systems.
Programming languages, computer logic, query/analysis tools and mathematics.
Microsoft Office 365, Visual Basic, PC applications and local area network are highly desirable.
Principles of budget monitoring.
Principles and practices of project management.
SCADA systems, process controls, programmable controllers and related computers.
Standards, practices, methods, materials, tools and measuring instruments used in the electrical, 
electronic and mechanical fields.
Principles and practices of safety management.
Pertinent local, State and Federal laws, codes, ordinances and regulations.
39 of 615

--- page 40 ---
Ability to:
Diagnose  electronic  malfunctions  and  defects  and  determine  parts,  adjustments  and repairs 
necessary for proper operation.
Read, understand and apply service manual instructions and electrical schematics. Use power and 
hand tools and measuring instruments, such as volt ohmmeter,
oscilloscope, tone test meter, simulator, transistor and capacitor checkers, phone handsets, frequency 
generator and similar instruments.
Use HMI graphic packages over LAN and WAN based SCADA network.
Work  effectively  with  computer  system  users,  department  staff,  third-party  software providers, staff 
at other cities for multi-city applications and contractors for specific projects.
Develop and implement various data collection and reporting systems.

maintenance and repair of industrial process control instrumentation.
Training:
Associate's degree or equivalent with course work in computer science, computer information systems 
or a related field or Associate’s degree or equivalent in electronics, instrumentation or a related field.

WASTEWATER TREATMENT PLANT MECHANIC III
DEFINITION
To perform highly skilled mechanical and minor electrical work in the installation, alteration, 
maintenance and repair of electrical systems, equipment and fixtures in a wastewater treatment plant; 
and to provide lead worker oversight as necessary; to do other related work as required.
DISTINGUISHING CHARACTERISTICS
This is an advanced journey level class in the Wastewater Treatment Plant Mechanic series. This 
position is distinguished from Wastewater Treatment Plant Mechanic I/II by its lead function and the 
degree of technical expertise required in performing the more complex work. This class may be 
responsible for lead supervision of Wastewater Treatment Plant Mechanics I/II.
SUPERVISION RECEIVED AND EXERCISED
Receives supervision and direction from the Wastewater Treatment Plant Maintenance 
Superintendent, or higher-level department personnel.  Technical or functional supervision may be 
provided by higher level treatment plant maintenance personnel.
Exercises lead supervision over other plant Maintenance Mechanics I/II, including provision of 
technical supervision, assignment of work and/or the training of less experienced mechanics.
EXAMPLES OF DUTIES
Participate in evaluating the activities of staff.
Depending upon assignment, duties may include, but are not limited to:
Read and interpret drawings, plans and diagrams of wastewater treatment plant infrastructure, 
equipment, controls and instrumentation.
Install, inspect, diagnose, and perform major and minor mechanical repair work on motors, pumps, 
boilers, hydraulic and pneumatic equipment, generators, filters, and other plant equipment.
42 of 615

--- page 43 ---
Use a variety of tools, hand tools, cutting and welding equipment and multi-meters.
Clean, oil and paint equipmentPerform preventive and corrective maintenance of all plant 
infrastructure particularly mechanical and electrical systems.
Evaluate equipment performance and identify problem areas. Assist with 
maintaining inventory and supply of replacement parts.
Perform general maintenance duties at the WWTP and Pump Station, including buildings, grounds and 
equipment as required.
Provide lead supervision and training to less experienced maintenance mechanic personnel and assure 
safe work practice and procedures are always being followed.
May be required to respond to emergency situations after normal working hours and be subject to 
serve on emergency stand-by alert and call out status on a rotational basis.
Adhere to purchasing policy.
Maintain a variety of chemical measuring equipment and plant process instrumentation.
Fully support and utilize the Computerized Maintenance Management System to maintain accurate 
records and reports of work performed.
Assist with installation of plant-related electrical systems, including but not limited to conduits, wires, 
relays, pull boxes, switch boards and switches required for additions, extensions or modifications to 
plant components.
OTHER JOB RELATED DUTIES:
Perform related duties and responsibilities as assigned.
JOB RELATED AND ESSENTIAL QUALIFICATIONS
Knowledge of:
Installation, diagnosis, operating and repair characteristics of wastewater plant mechanical and 
electrical systems, including repairing and maintaining motors, pumps, boilers, hydraulic equipment, 
generators, and other plant equipment.
Particular safety hazards associated with wastewater plant operations and the discharge hazards and 
conditions that may result from mechanical malfunctions.
General principles of supervision and on-the-job training.
43 of 615

--- page 44 ---
General principles and practices of project management.
Standard practices, materials, tools and equipment used in the mechanical or electrical repair of 
minor and major mechanical equipment systems.
Safe driving principles and practices to operate motor vehicles safely, including but not limited to 
fork lifts, service and boom trucks, and standard passenger vehicles.
Use of Asset Management and Computerized Maintenance Management Systems.
Ability to:
Perform advanced level mechanical maintenance work including the troubleshooting of equipment for 
both minor and major repair work.
Read and interpret blueprints, sketches and diagrams.
Use and care for tools and equipment of the electrical trade. Develop and 
maintain accurate maintenance records.
Communicate clearly and concisely, both orally and in writing.
Establish, maintain and foster positive and harmonious working relationships with those contacted in 
the course of work.
Lead, train and provide input in evaluation of Plant Mechanics.
Ability to use computers and electronic communications device in the course of work. Assist in the 
preparation of Administrative Reports.
Diagnose, locate, and adjust defects in electrical and mechanical systems and equipment.
Experience and Training Guidelines:
Any combination equivalent to experience and training that would provide the required knowledge, 
skills, and abilities would be qualifying. A typical way to obtain the knowledge, skills, and abilities 
would be:
Experience:
Minimum  four  years  of  experience  involving  Journey  Level  skilled  mechanical  and electrical 
maintenance on major equipment and systems.
OR
Two years of experience performing duties comparable to those of a Wastewater Treatment Plant

DEFINITION
To perform semi-skilled and skilled maintenance and repair work on municipal buildings, facilities and 
equipment; and related work as assigned.
SUPERVISION RECEIVED AND EXERCISED
General supervision is provided by the Building Maintenance Supervisor or Wastewater Treatment Plant 
Maintenance Superintendent when assigned to the Wastewater Treatment Plant, or higher-level 
department personnel.
Responsibilities may include some technical or functional assistance to semi-skilled maintenance 
workers.
EXAMPLES OF DUTIES
Depending upon assignment, duties may include, but are not limited to a variety of routine and 
semiskilled tasks:

Performs a variety of facility maintenance tasks involving some or all of the following skill 
trades; plumbing, electrical, carpentry, HVAC, painting, and preventative maintenance 
scheduling.

Installs, repairs, and maintains lavatories, sinks, toilets, urinals, wash basins, drains, gas and 
electric heaters/ water heaters, water filters, water pumps, boilers, HVAC units, water valves, 
drinking fountains, and other plumbing systems.

Repairs and maintains playground equipment, fabrication using arc welding, brazing and 
acetylene.

Performs electrical repair such as installing light fixtures, light tubes, outlets, switches, and 
ballasts.

Maintains swimming pool and associated chemical/pumping equipment.

Performs maintenance on electrical, heating, air conditioning, motorized equipment, generators, 
and building alarm/building management systems.

Maintains, repair, and trouble shoot appliances; such as refrigerators, stoves, ovens, and 
dishwashers, etc.



skilled maintenance technician/worker. Specific duties performed may vary by assignment. 
This class performs related work as required.

JUNIOR ENGINEER
ASSISTANT ENGINEER
ASSOCIATE ENGINEER
DEFINITION
To perform professional engineering work in the design, investigation, and construction of public works 
and related projects; to act as project manager in assigned capital projects; and to do related work as 
required.
DISTINGUISHING CHARACTERISTICS
Junior Engineer - This is the entry-level class in the professional engineering series.  This class is 
distinguished from the Assistant Engineer by the performance of the more routine tasks and duties 
assigned to positions within the series.  Incumbents perform less complex office and field engineering 
work and assignments are generally routine or closely related within a framework of established 
procedures.  Since this class is typically used as a training class, employees may have only limited or no 
directly related work experience.
Assistant Engineer - This is the journey level class in the professional engineering series not requiring 
registration.  Positions in this class are normally filled by advancement from the lower class of Junior 
Engineer.  Incumbents of this class perform the moderately difficult professional engineering work.  
Assistant Engineers are normally considered to be on a continuing training status and as assigned 
responsibilities and breadth of knowledge increase with experience and registration is received, may 
reasonably expect their positions to be reassigned to the next higher class of Associate Engineer.
Associate Engineer - This is the journey level class within the professional engineering series and 
requires registration.  Employees within this class are distinguished from the Assistant Engineer by the 
greater complexity of the assignments received and by the greater independence with which an incumbent 
is expected to operate.  Incumbents are expected to complete complex engineering assignments requiring 
the use of judgment and initiative in developing solutions to problems, interpreting general policies, and 
determining work assignments.  Employees at this level receive only occasional instruction or assistance 
as new or unusual situations arise, and are fully aware of the operating procedures and policies of the work 
unit.  Positions in the class are flexibly staffed and are normally filled by advancement from the lower 
class of Assistant Engineer, or when filled from the outside, require prior advanced technical or 
professional engineering work experience.
49 of 615

--- page 50 ---
SUPERVISION RECEIVED AND EXERCISED
Junior Engineer
Receives general supervision from high level professional engineers.
May exercise technical or functional supervision over technical engineering staff.
Assistant Engineer
Receives general supervision from higher level professional engineers.
May exercise technical or functional supervision over technical engineering staff.
Associate Engineer
Receives direction from higher level engineering staff.
May exercise technical supervision over lower level staff within the work unit and exercises technical or 
functional supervision over technical engineering staff.
EXAMPLES OF DUTIES - Depending upon assignment, duties may include, but are not limited to, the 
following:
Design and prepare plans and specifications and cost estimates for public works projects including 
pumping stations, streets, storm drains, sewer lines, and related projects.
Research project design requirements; perform complex calculations and prepare estimates of time and 
material costs.
Design and prepare plans and specifications for the installation of traffic signals; traffic control devices, 
and street lighting systems.
Review field projects being studied, designed or constructed.
Assign routine investigation, design and drafting tasks to technical subordinates; request 
survey, mapping and data collection; review completed work; assist in the solution of difficult problems.
Research publications and industry information sources to remain current with modern developments, 
principles and procedures.
Develop revised design and construction standards for public works structures and appurtenances.
Investigate field problems affecting property owners, contractors, and maintenance operations.
Prepare estimates and feasibility reports for new or modified services and structures.
Design and maintain spreadsheets and databases on mainframe and personal computers; write and 
50 of 615

--- page 51 ---
maintain special programs on personal computers.
Fully utilize the computerized work systems in accordance with work flow processes.
Participate in the planning, checking, review, and processing of plans for private developments affecting 
city streets, sewers, drains and related public works facilities and ensure that they
meet all City-imposed requirements; analyze materials and design data submitted to the City.
Prepare special engineering studies and reports.
Prepare various applications for state and federal grants.
Administer and enforce City Codes and standards on engineering projects.
Determine conformance of parcel and subdivision maps and improvement plans to City requirements; 
maintain status of parcel and subdivision maps.
Serve as staff to a variety of City commissions, boards, and committees on public works or traffic 
engineering matters.
Coordinate public works activities with other City departments, divisions and sections and with outside 
agencies.
May supervise and train professional and technical staff.
Provide staff assistance to the Department Head or Division Manager, including special projects, complex 
project management, reports, prepare staff reports, and represent the City at City Council meetings.
Maintain current knowledge of new trends and innovations in the professional fields, participate in 
professional groups.
Research, negotiate, manage select and monitor contracts with vendors.
Advise the public or other city staff in processing transactions in the area his/her professional expertise or 
maneuvering city policies or responds to inquiries and requests by public, departments, press, or other 
department staff.
Develop and/or deliver high level materials, trainings, or presentations widely disseminated to the City or 
the public.
Manage large scale projects that span long time horizons and require high level expertise.
Process city transactions.
Perform other duties as assigned.
QUALIFICATIONS
51 of 615

engineering projects.

Make complex engineering computations, and check, design, and prepare engineering plans, studies, 
profiles, and maps.
Use and care for drafting and surveying instruments.
Use computers and handheld electronic devices in the course of work.
